name: postmarker

metadata.name: postmarker
metadata.summary: Python client library for Postmark API

documentation: --- substitutions: Build: |- ```{image} https://github.com/Stranger6667/postmarker/workflows/build/badge.svg :target: https://github.com/Stranger6667/postmarker/actions ``` Chat: |- ```{image} https://img.shields.io/gitter/room/Stranger6667/postmarker.svg :alt: Gitter :target: https://gitter.im/Stranger6667/postmarker ``` Coverage: |- ```{image} https://codecov.io/github/Stranger6667/postmarker/coverage.svg?branch=master :target: https://codecov.io/github/Stranger6667/postmarker?branch=master ``` Docs: |- ```{image} https://readthedocs.org/projects/postmarker/badge/?version=stable :alt: Documentation Status :target: https://postmarker.readthedocs.io/en/stable/ ``` License: |- ```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/l/postmarker.svg :target: https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT ``` Python versions: |- ```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/postmarker.svg :target: https://pypi.org/project/postmarker/ ``` Version: |- ```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/postmarker.svg :target: https://pypi.org/project/postmarker/ ``` --- # Postmarker {{ Build }} {{ Coverage }} {{ Version }} {{ Python versions }} {{ Docs }} {{ Chat }} {{ License }} Python client library for [Postmark API](http://developer.postmarkapp.com/developer-api-overview.html). **Gitter**: # Installation Postmarker can be obtained with `pip`: ``` $ pip install postmarker ``` # Usage example **NOTE**: The attributes of all classes are provided **as is**, without transformation to snake case. We don't want to introduce new names for existing entities. Send single email: ```python >>> from postmarker.core import PostmarkClient >>> postmark = PostmarkClient(server_token='API_TOKEN') >>> postmark.emails.send( From='sender@example.com', To='receiver@example.com', Subject='Postmark test', HtmlBody='Hello dear Postmark user.' ) ``` Send batch: ```python >>> postmark.emails.send_batch( { 'From': 'sender@example.com', 'To': 'receiver@example.com', 'Subject': 'Postmark test', 'HtmlBody': 'Hello dear Postmark user.', }, { 'From': 'sender2@example.com', 'To': 'receiver2@example.com', 'Subject': 'Postmark test 2', 'HtmlBody': 'Hello dear Postmark user.', } ) ``` Setup an email: ```python >>> email = postmark.emails.Email( From='sender@example.com', To='receiver@example.com', Subject='Postmark test', HtmlBody='Hello dear Postmark user.' ) >>> email['X-Accept-Language'] = 'en-us, en' >>> email.attach('/home/user/readme.txt') >>> email.attach_binary(content=b'content', filename='readme.txt') >>> email.send() ``` There are a lot of features available. Check it out in our documentation! Here's just a few of them: - Support for sending Python email instances. - Bounces, Domains, Messages, Templates, Sender signatures, Status, Stats & Server API. - Django email backend. - Tornado helper. - Spam check API. - Wrappers for Bounce, Inbound, Open and Delivery webhooks. # Documentation You can view the documentation online at: - Or you can look at the docs/ directory in the repository. # Python support Postmarker supports CPython 3.6 - 3.10 and PyPy 3.7 & 3.8. # Thanks Many thanks to [Shmele](https://github.com/butorov) and [lobziik](https://github.com/lobziik) for their reviews and advice :)

classes.name: postmarker.models.server.ServerManager
documentation: Lets you get or edit details of the specific server.

methods.name: postmarker.models.stats.StatsManager.overview
documentation: Gets a brief overview of statistics for all of your outbound email.

methods.name: postmarker.models.stats.StatsManager.sends
documentation: Gets a total count of emails you’ve sent out.

methods.name: postmarker.models.stats.StatsManager.bounces
documentation: Gets total counts of emails you’ve sent out that have been returned as bounced.

methods.name: postmarker.models.stats.StatsManager.spam
documentation: Gets a total count of recipients who have marked your email as spam.

methods.name: postmarker.models.stats.StatsManager.tracked
documentation: Gets a total count of emails you’ve sent with open tracking or link tracking enabled.

methods.name: postmarker.models.stats.StatsManager.opens
documentation:
Gets total counts of recipients who opened your emails.

This is only recorded when open tracking is enabled for that email.

methods.name: postmarker.models.stats.StatsManager.opens_platforms
documentation:
Gets an overview of the platforms used to open your emails.

This is only recorded when open tracking is enabled for that email.

methods.name: postmarker.models.stats.StatsManager.emailclients
documentation:
Gets an overview of the email clients used to open your emails.

This is only recorded when open tracking is enabled for that email.

methods.name: postmarker.models.stats.StatsManager.readtimes
documentation:
Gets the length of time that recipients read emails along with counts for each time.

This is only recorded when open tracking is enabled for that email.
Read time tracking stops at 20 seconds, so any read times above that will appear in the 20s+ field.

methods.name: postmarker.models.stats.StatsManager.clicks
documentation: Gets total counts of unique links that were clicked.

methods.name: postmarker.models.stats.StatsManager.browserfamilies
documentation:
Gets an overview of the browsers used to open links in your emails.

This is only recorded when Link Tracking is enabled for that email.

methods.name: postmarker.models.stats.StatsManager.clicks_platforms
documentation:
Gets an overview of the browser platforms used to open your emails.

This is only recorded when Link Tracking is enabled for that email.

methods.name: postmarker.models.stats.StatsManager.location
documentation:
Gets an overview of which part of the email links were clicked from (HTML or Text).

This is only recorded when Link Tracking is enabled for that email.

modules.name: postmarker.models.triggers
documentation: pylint: disable=redefined-builtin

methods.name: postmarker.models.messages.OutboundMessageManager.all
documentation:
All outbound messages.

Lets you get all the details about any outbound or inbound message that you sent or received
through a specific server. Messages expire after 45 days.

```
param int count

Number of messages to return per request.

param int offset

Number of messages to skip.

param str recipient

Filter by the user who was receiving the email.

param str fromemail

Filter by the sender email address.

param str tag

Filter by tag.

param str status

Filter by status (queued or sent).

param date todate

Filter messages up to the date specified (inclusive).

param date fromdate

Filter messages starting from the date specified (inclusive).

param subject

Filter by email subject.

return

A list of  instances.

rtype

```

methods.name: postmarker.models.messages.InboundMessageManager.all
documentation:
All inbound messages.

```
param count

Number of messages to return per request.

param offset

Number of messages to skip.

param recipient

Filter by the user who was receiving the email.

param fromemail

Filter by the sender email address.

param tag

Filter by tag.

param subject

Filter by email subject.

param mailboxhash

Filter by mailboxhash.

param status

Filter by status (blocked, processed, queued, failed, scheduled).

param date todate

Filter messages up to the date specified (inclusive).

param date fromdate

Filter messages starting from the date specified (inclusive).

return

A list of  instances.

rtype

```

modules.name: postmarker.models.bounces
documentation:
Bounces.

Information about bounces is available via the {py:class}`~postmarker.models.bounces.BounceManager`,
which is an attribute of `postmark` instance.

methods.name: postmarker.models.emails.EmailManager.send
documentation:
Sends a single email.

```
param message

 or email.mime.text.MIMEText instance.

param str From

The sender email address.

param To

Recipient's email address.
Multiple recipients could be specified as a list or string with comma separated values.

type To

str or list

param Cc

Cc recipient's email address.
Multiple Cc recipients could be specified as a list or string with comma separated values.

type Cc

str or list

param Bcc

Bcc recipient's email address.
Multiple Bcc recipients could be specified as a list or string with comma separated values.

type Bcc

str or list

param str Subject

Email subject.

param str Tag

Email tag.

param str HtmlBody

HTML email message.

param str TextBody

Plain text email message.

param str ReplyTo

Reply To override email address.

param dict Headers

Dictionary of custom headers to include.

param bool TrackOpens

Activate open tracking for this email.

param str TrackLinks

Activate link tracking for links in the HTML or Text bodies of this email.

param list Attachments

List of attachments.

return

Information about sent email.

rtype

```

methods.name: postmarker.core.PostmarkClient._setup_managers
documentation:
Allows to access manager by model name.

>>> postmark.bounces

Basic class for API clients. Provides basic functionality to make requests.
